---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# ggsegHammersmith

<!-- badges: start -->
[![R-CMD-check](https://github.com/ggsegverse/ggsegHammersmith/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/ggsegverse/ggsegHammersmith/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml)
[![r-universe](https://ggsegverse.r-universe.dev/badges/ggsegHammersmith)](https://ggsegverse.r-universe.dev/ggsegHammersmith)
<!-- badges: end -->

Hammersmith Brain Atlas for the ggsegverse Ecosystem.

## Installation

```{r, eval = FALSE}
# From r-universe
install.packages("ggsegHammersmith", repos = "https://ggsegverse.r-universe.dev")

# From GitHub
# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("ggsegverse/ggsegHammersmith")
```

## Atlases
### hammersmith
 
Hammersmith maximum probability map with 83 regions.

```{r hammersmith}
library(ggsegHammersmith)
plot(hammersmith())
```
## Data source

[brain-development.org](http://brain-development.org/brain-atlases/adult-brain-atlases/) (requires license acceptance).

- **Reference**: Hammers et al. (2003) [doi:10.1016/S1053-8119(03)00165-7](https://doi.org/10.1016/S1053-8119(03)00165-7)

- **Date obtained**: 2026-03-28
